attributes.action: An Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty at Kirklees Park & expanded protection
attributes.additional_details:
Yes, Parliament can create protected areas. In the UK, Parliament establishes protected areas—also known as designated sites—through legislation to protect nature, countryside, and marine habitats. 

Key details regarding Parliament and protected areas include:
Legislative Power: Acts of Parliament, such as the Environment Act 2021 and various marine acts, provide the legal basis for creating and managing protected areas, including  Areas of Outstanding Natural Beauty (AONBs(
attributes.background:
We want a special protected area established in law, For Kirklees Park to extend and protect the area around the prehistoric site for future generations.

This not private bills concerning land. It is firmly in the public buisness and we need to take a legislative initiative
